rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical 1,2-dialkylation of alkenes with a tertiary alkyl bromide (tBuBr) and a primary alkyl bromide via radical addition/cathodic reduction to a carbanion, followed by SN2 alkylation.
electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction generates a tert-butyl radical from tBuBr; after radical addition to the alkene, the resulting benzylic/stabilized C-radical is further reduced at the cathode to a carbanion, which then undergoes SN2 with the primary alkyl bromide. Mg sacrificial anode balances charge.

key_intermediates.name: tert-butyl radical from cathodic reduction of tBuBr
benzylic/stabilized C-radical after tBu addition to alkene
ring-opened homoallyl radical from cyclopropane radical clock 59

electron_flow: Cathode reduces tBuBr to tBu radical, which adds to the alkene; the resulting stabilized C-radical is reduced again at the cathode to a carbanion that performs SN2 on the primary alkyl bromide. The Mg anode is sacrificially oxidized to balance charge.

probe_reagents.name: (2-phenylcyclopropyl)ethylene radical clock 59
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened product 60 (1-chloro-9,9-dimethyldec-6-en-4-yl)benzene isolated in 18% (7:1 E/Z), confirming a carbon radical intermediate after tBu addition.

probe_reagents.name: D2O
probe_reagents.observation: Product 61 obtained in 8% yield with 82% D incorporation at the benzylic position, consistent with a carbanion intermediate quenched by D2O.

notes: DFT (M06-2X/6-311+G(d,p), SMD(DME)) supports exergonic, mostly irreversible tBu• addition to π-conjugated alkenes (ΔG‡ ≈ 10.4–12.6 kcal/mol) and an SN2 mechanism for the second C–C bond formation with the primary alkyl bromide; the radical/anion-stabilizing nature of group Z dictates reactivity, with vinyl silanes/phosphines/sulfides being less reactive.
